name: Finland
contextBlurb: As of 2025, Finland applies the EU’s MiCA framework to stablecoins and crypto-asset services. The Finnish Financial Supervisory Authority (FIN-FSA) is the national competent authority, authorising and supervising CASPs and MiCA-compliant stablecoin issuers. Finland opted for a 6-month transition that ended on 30 June 2025, after which only MiCA-authorised firms may operate. Under MiCA, EMTs (single-fiat tokens) carry a par-value redemption right and strict safeguarding; ARTs (basket-referenced) face reserve, disclosure and governance duties, with additional measures for “significant” tokens.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
Finland’s national transition period ends; national regime repealed and replaced by MiCA—only authorised CASPs may operate.
Updated FIN-FSA AML/CFT Regulations & Guidelines 2/2023 enter into force.
FIN-FSA says firms must obtain CASP authorisation to continue after 30 Jun; eight applications received.
MiCA becomes fully applicable; Finland confirms a 6-month national transition to 30 Jun 2025.
MiCA’s stablecoin rules (Titles III & IV for ARTs/EMTs) start applying across the EU, covering issuance, reserves and redemption.

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: As of 2025, Finland applies the EU’s MiCA framework to stablecoins and crypto-asset services. The Finnish Financial Supervisory Authority (FIN-FSA) is the national competent authority, authorising and supervising CASPs and MiCA-compliant stablecoin issuers. Finland opted for a 6-month transition that ended on 30 June 2025, after which only MiCA-authorised firms may operate. Under MiCA, EMTs (single-fiat tokens) carry a par-value redemption right and strict safeguarding; ARTs (basket-referenced) face reserve, disclosure and governance duties, with additional measures for “significant” tokens.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Under MiCA, stablecoins in Finland are either Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs) or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs). EMTs are pegged 1:1 to a single official currency (e.g., EUR) and may be issued only by credit institutions or e-money institutions, with statutory safeguarding and redemption obligations;

regulators.name: Finnish Financial Supervisory Authority
regulators.role: The primary regulator for stablecoins and crypto-assets in Finland, responsible for authorizing and supervising crypto-asset service providers (CASPs) under MiCA. It enforces licensing, AML, consumer
